ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for butterflied pork steak; closest match is pork steak (raw). Butterfly cut and lean-only specification are not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers raw pig meat broadly; no specific butterfly-cut or lean-only sub-code exists. Used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for butterflied lean pork steak raw; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: pork loin or leg muscle meat

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw pork butterfly steak portion is approximately 150 g, representing one steak as purchased before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Pork, fresh, loin, top loin (chops), boneless, separable lean only, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient values sourced from Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id F06090025, confidence 0.95) for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for boneless lean raw pork loin used to supplement saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and additional micronutrients where HPB data was not available. The butterfly cut is a preparation technique (splitting and flattening) that does not alter the nutritional composition of the lean muscle meat itself.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g per 100 g). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. No GI value is assigned. glycemic_index is set to -1 (sentinel: not applicable / not measurable) consistent with numeric hygiene requirements; gi_category is set to not_applicable.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (46 mg/100 g) and high potassium (420 mg/100 g) yield an excellent sodium-to-potassium ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension may consume lean raw pork butterfly steak freely as a protein source, provided it is cooked without added salt or high-sodium marinades, to preserve its naturally low-sodium, high-potassium nutritional profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content with 24.1 g/100 g high-quality protein and minimal fat (1.6 g/100 g) produce no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lean pork butterfly steak is an excellent protein choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as its zero carbohydrate content causes no postprandial blood glucose rise; pair with non-starchy vegetables and a small low-GI grain portion for a nutritionally complete meal, and avoid sugar-containing marinades or glazes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (1.6 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (~0.55 g/100 g) with high protein and low sodium support a heart-healthy dietary pattern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This extremely lean pork cut is suitable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease when prepared by grilling or baking without added saturated fats or salt; its low saturated fat content is consistent with AHA dietary guidelines for heart health, though red meat frequency should remain moderate (no more than 3–4 servings per week).

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork is a red meat containing arachidonic acid (omega-6), which can amplify pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6) relevant to rheumatoid arthritis pathophysiology
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat including lean pork to no more than once weekly, substitute with anti-inflammatory proteins such as fatty fish, chicken breast, or turkey where possible, and pair any pork consumption with anti-inflammatory foods such as turmeric, ginger, and omega-3-rich sides.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork butterfly steak, raw and lean, is a boneless pork cut that has been butterflied — sliced open horizontally and flattened to create a uniform, thin steak shape — with all visible internal and external separable fat removed. The raw meat displays a pale pink colour with a firm, dense texture characteristic of lean pork muscle. Per 100 g, it provides 112 kcal, 24.1 g of high-quality complete protein, only 1.6 g of total fat (of which approximately 0.55 g is saturated fat), and zero carbohydrate and sugar, making it one of the leanest pork cuts available. Sodium is very low at 46 mg per 100 g, while potassium is high at 420 mg per 100 g, yielding an outstanding sodium-to-potassium ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles for blood pressure management. Cholesterol is moderate at 48 mg per 100 g. The butterfly cut is a Western butchery technique commonly used to increase surface area for even cooking, marinating, or stuffing. It is suitable for grilling, pan-searing, or baking. Clinically, this cut is highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero glycaemic impact), hypertension (low sodium, high potassium), and cardiovascular disease (very low saturated fat). It is not recommended as a primary protein for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to arachidonic acid content in red meat. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. Halal status depends on slaughter method and certification.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id F06090025, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Supplementary micronutrient values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for boneless lean raw pork loin, which is the closest approved-source equivalent. The butterfly cut is a preparation technique that does not alter the nutritional composition of the lean muscle meat. All core macros are consistent between HPB and USDA data.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (F06090025); this is naturally zero for plain lean pork muscle meat with no added ingredients. Sugar_g is naturally zero for plain lean pork. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a pure animal muscle meat product. Trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as lean pork contains negligible industrially produced trans fats; naturally occurring trans fats in ruminant meat are not applicable to pork. Saturated_fat_g (0.55 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.62 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.16 g), and B-vitamin values (thiamine 0.74 mg, riboflavin 0.22 mg, niacin 6.3 mg, B6 0.54 mg, B12 0.54 mcg), phosphorus (230 mg), magnesium (26 mg), zinc (1.8 mg), selenium (32 mcg), and water_g (73.5 g) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork loin, boneless, separable lean only, raw (FDC ID 168313), as HPB does not report these fields. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_e_mg are set to -1 as these are not reliably reported for lean raw pork in either HPB or USDA SR Legacy at the level of precision required. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g per 100 g); glycemic_index is set to -1 (numeric sentinel indicating not applicable / not measurable for this food type) and gi_category to not_applicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. Halal status is not confirmed as it depends on slaughter method and certification; is_halal is set to false pending certification verification. The butterfly cut is a butchery preparation technique and does not alter nutritional composition.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for pork steak raw (closest_parent match, gap noted). FoodEx2 code A0CKP for raw pig meat provided (closest_parent match, gap noted). SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented. At least one defensible ontology anchor present.
